description: End-to-end agent loop: an AI coding agent picks up a Jira ticket, uses Jira as execution context, implements it, and opens a PR linked back to the ticket. The coding and PR-creation themselves are outside Jira — same cost both arms, omitted here. Steps 0-2 use measured per-op benchmarks; steps 3-6 use conservative estimates (no Jira mutations performed). Every step is a standalone number — swap in your own measurements to refine.
scenario: Agent receives: 'Work on BENCH-1 and open a PR linked to it.' The Jira interactions that make up the loop:

steps.label: List related assigned tickets (context for blockers)
Transition To Do -> In Progress
steps.source: estimate (small write: bash command vs tool-call envelope)

notes: Estimates for steps 3-6 are conservative; MCP may be higher in practice because addCommentToJiraIssue can return the created comment object with full metadata.

An 'MCP-optimized' variant where the agent always sets contentFormat: markdown + fields projection would narrow the ratio to ~3x on writes, but the read-path gap (step 2, 10 KB per search) doesn't close.
Reasoning tokens between tool calls are not counted; they scale roughly with tool-result size, so the true end-to-end gap is likely a bit larger.
